channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: New DOJ Social Media Policy Restricts Employees' Online Activity
item.description: DOJ employees restricted from posting on social media related to government work, sparking concerns over free speech and potential targeting of career public servants
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Hertz says hackers stole customer data in vendor breach
item.description: Hertz is notifying customers that hackers may have stolen personal information including credit card details and Social Security numbers during a data breach on one of its vendors.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second say of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notion Mail lets you build the inbox you actually want
item.description: Most email apps have strong opinions about what your inbox should look like. Notion Mail is the opposite. The new offshoot from productivity app Notion is all about flexibility.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: Google got hit with a cease and desist order in Japan. Here’s why
item.description: Japanese regulators on Tuesday accused U.S. tech giant Google of violating anti-monopoly laws, echoing similar moves in the U.S. and Europe. Google Japan said in a statement that it found the action “regrettable.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Grassley presses Zuckerberg over Meta's treatment of whistleblower
item.description: Senate Judiciary Committee Chair Chuck Grassley (R-Iowa) sent a letter to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg on Monday asking him to address alleged attempts to silence a former employee who approached his office with whistleblower allegations against the company.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation.
item.dc:creator: Brian Witte, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: What ‘Ex Machina’ got right (and wrong) about AI, 10 years later
item.description: Before media outlets began comparing OpenAI’s Sam Altman with the father of the atomic bomb, and before Amazon’s Jeff Bezos got jacked, we had Nathan Bateman, the iron-pumping, AI-developing tech broligarch played by Oscar Isaac in the 2015 film Ex Machina.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Visa cancellations sow panic for international students, with hundreds fearing deportation
item.description: The speed and scope of the federal government’s efforts to terminate the legal status of international students have stunned colleges and universities across the country.
item.dc:creator: Annie Ma, Makiya Seminera And Christopher L. Keller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cato Networks augments CASB with genAI security
item.description: Cato Networks recently unveiled new generative AI capabilities in its Cloud Access Security Broker (CASB) that the secure access service edge (SASE) provider says will let enterprise IT organizations detect, analyze, and gain insights into the use of genAI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hello, Flipper! Google has developed a genAI model to talk to dolphins
item.description: Google has unveiled a new generative AI (genAI) model, Dolphin Gemma, which will attempt to decode how dolphins communicate with each other. Dolphins are among the most intelligent animals on the planet and communicate partly through whistles and calls.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will let players repair their gear, but only if they download an app
item.description: The Nintendo Switch 2 versions of Breath of the Wild and Tears of the Kingdom will give players the ability to repair their equipment with a companion app.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: A major Google Cloud outage was caused by uninterruptible power supplies being...interrupted
item.description: Google had a “critical battery failure” that took its Columbus, Ohio zone offline for up to six hours on March 29.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Starship blooper: Windows 10 update gets weirdest bug yet
item.description: Bugs in the bugs: Windows 10 update plasters ‘error install failure’ message over your screen when the upgrade has actually installed just fine.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: 2025 global network outage report and internet health check
item.description: The reliability of services delivered by ISPs, cloud providers and conferencing services is critical for enterprise organizations.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Pig Liver "Bridge" Study Aims to Save Human Lives
item.description: US researchers are set to test the use of gene-edited pig livers in treating sudden liver failure.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Prime Video's most chaotic femme fatale is back for Another Simple Favor and I can't wait to find out her deadly secret
item.description: The trailer for Another Simple Favor looks like Prime Video might have another streaming hit on its hands.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Trade routes of the digital age: How data gravity shapes cloud strategy
item.description: Throughout history, the movement of goods, knowledge and influence has been shaped by gravitational forces — not those of planets, but of human civilization. One of the most striking examples is the Silk Road, a vast network of trade routes that connected the East and West for centuries.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: X Under Fire: European Regulatory Probe Targets Public Post Use
item.description: Elon Musk's X faces regulatory probe over alleged use of public posts from EU users to train Grok AI chatbot, potentially setting precedent for data usage under EU privacy laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Transforming the WAN to future-proof the network infrastructure
item.description: Enterprise network infrastructures have evolved significantly alongside the rise of cloud computing, Software-as-a-Service (SaaS), and AI.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Los CIO recalibran las agendas de TI para hacer sitio al aumento del gasto en IA
item.description: Los directores ejecutivos, sus consejos y el resto de la alta dirección siguen impulsando la inteligencia artificial (IA) como un imperativo estratégico.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Elon Musk and Jack Dorsey want to kill IP law. That would be a huge mistake
item.description: Few statements communicate a sentiment more directly than the four words Jack Dorsey, a cofounder of Twitter, posted over the weekend on the platform he no longer controls: “delete all IP law,” was his simple message.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft releases out-of-band updates to fix reporting error
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency patches to fix an apparent reporting error in Active Directory (AD) Group Policy, which allows administrators to manage and configure user and computer settings in Windows.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg takes the stand in historic antitrust trial
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg took to the witness stand on the first day of a historic antitrust trial to defend his company against allegations it illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Meta says it will resume AI training with public content from European users
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Social media company Meta said Monday that it will start using publicly available content from European users to train its artificial intelligence models, resuming work put on hold last year after activists raised concerns about data privacy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: As legacy of Ford fades in Brazil, China's BYD looks to extend global lead in EV sales
item.description: CAMACARI, Brazil (AP) — When Chinese automaker BYD Co. announced plans to build a massive factory in the hardscrabble city of Camacari, in Brazil’s northeastern Bahia state, locals saw a new beginning. After years of economic stagnation following Ford Motor Co.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: How pet projects fuel innovation and careers in tech
item.description: Call them pet projects, side projects, or hobby projects, projects pursued outside of one’s regular business activity are especially popular among software developers. After all, these projects serve as an excellent way to learn new skills and technologies or to test fresh concepts and approaches.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (AP) — Like many in the tech industry, Jeremy Lyons used to think of himself as a relatively apolitical guy.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A multicloud experiment in agentic AI: Lessons learned
item.description: Recently I undertook a project to design and validate agentic AI architectures capable of operating autonomously across various public cloud providers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Hackers breach Morocco's social security database in an unprecedented cyberattack
item.description: RABAT, Morocco (AP) — Morocco 's social security agency said troves of data were stolen from its systems in a cyberattack this week that resulted in personal information being leaked on the messaging app Telegram.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: OpenAI countersues Elon Musk in legal dispute over ChatGPT maker's business ambitions
item.description: OpenAI is suing Elon Musk for unfair competition and interfering with its business relationships with investors and customers, escalating a legal battle between the ChatGPT maker and the billionaire who helped bankroll the artificial intelligence startup a decade ago.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies
item.description: Gina Scinta, the deputy CTO at Thales Trusted Cyber Technologies, says new survey data provides insights for how agencies better secure data and networks. The post New federal data threats demand new mitigation technologies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Humanoid robot breakdances its way into history
item.description: Atlas robot by Boston Dynamics amazes tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson with its breakdancing moves.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft fires employees who protested its support for Israel
item.description: Two Microsoft employees were fired for protests condemning the company’s support for Israel at a 50th anniversary event last week.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation
